economic_notes: First Nation has an outstanding treaty benefits claim against the Government of Canada initiated in 2020, financed by RBC loan of $1,598,607. Two business entities: Canoe Lake Resource Development Inc. and Eagles Lake Community Store LP accounted for by modified equity method. Multiple capital infrastructure projects in progress including water treatment plant upgrade, solid waste transfer station, roadway and drainage upgrades.
notes: Fiscal year 2022 (ended March 31, 2022), not 2024. Province is Saskatchewan. Auditor emphasis of matter paragraph regarding outstanding treaty benefits claim against Government of Canada financed by RBC loan ($1,598,607). CMHC Housing Program consolidated as subsidiary. Two GBEs (Canoe Lake Resource Development Inc. and Eagles Lake Community Store LP) accounted for by modified equity method. Capital lease obligations of $756,846 relate to Brandt Tractor equipment leases. Sales-type lease receivable of $756,846 to Canoe Lake Resource Development Inc. First Nation not in compliance with all loan covenants. Total government transfers calculated as ISC ($15,736,092) + MLTC Program Services ($3,268,649) + Meadow Lake Health & Social Development Authority ($2,433,639) + CMHC ($450,629) + MLTC Child & Family Services ($478,464) + Government of Saskatchewan ($23,747) - some may be tribal council pass-throughs rather than direct government transfers. OSR estimated as remaining revenue lines excluding government transfers.
